query:
1. Per-profile independence — yes.                         
  Mixed sections would be misleading. If a run includes both
  distribution-changing and stable profiles, the reader needs
   to see each profile's gate on its own terms. A global   
  Section 5 that flattens them hides whether the             
  distribution-changing profile actually passed. Per-profile
  is the only honest rendering.

  2. Version field — yes, include it.                        
  Costs nothing now. The alternative is later parsing "absent
   field = version 1" which is always messier than an        
  explicit field. Start at "version": 1.                   
                                                             
  3. MRR floor is a veto, not a co-equal target.             
  The semantics are different even if the outcome is the same
   today. CC@k and ICD measure whether diversity improved.   
  MRR floor measures whether ranking quality didn't collapse.
   They're orthogonal: you can improve diversity by shuffling
   the ranking and tank MRR. The floor framing —           
  "distribution gate passes only if MRR doesn't fall below
  threshold" — is the accurate one. Report wording should
  reflect this: distribution gate result first, then MRR
  floor: PASS/FAIL as a separate line that can independently
  block. This also makes it easier to diagnose failures —
  "diversity targets met, but ranking regressed" is a
  different problem than "diversity targets not met."

  4. run_report reads profile-meta.json itself.
  The results directory is the artifact boundary — everything
   needed to reproduce the report should live there. If      
  run_eval_command loads and passes it, you've created a
  coupling where the report can't be re-rendered from the    
  results dir alone (e.g., CI artifacts, post-hoc analysis).
  Option (a) keeps the report self-contained.

entries.title: ADR-003 nan-008: Round-Trip Integration Test Guards Dual Type Copy and Section Order
ADR-003: One Composite Stewardship Check Per Gate
ADR-005 nan-010: Per-Profile Section 5 Rendering with Independent Gating
Differential profile tests require extreme input values to expose weight-wiring bugs reliably
